bugs.root_cause: 2 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 5→5 lines (2 removed)

bugs.error_message: Root test task only ran :backend:test; ./gradlew test skipped all frontend jsTest targets
bugs.root_cause: Root test task only delegated to :backend:test; :frontend:jsTest was omitted in the initial multi-module fix
bugs.fix: Added ":frontend:jsTest" to dependsOn in root tasks.register("test") call

bugs.error_message: (node) [DEP0169] DeprecationWarning: url.parse() ... [DEP0040] DeprecationWarning: punycode module deprecated
bugs.root_cause: Kotlin/JS uses yarn 1.22.17 internally which calls url.parse() and punycode APIs deprecated in Node.js 22. kotlin.js.yarn=false is NOT supported in Kotlin 2.3.20 (yarn tasks still run). KotlinNpmInstallTask extends DefaultTask not Exec so environment() is unavailable. YarnEnvironment has no env-var map. Only fix is injecting NODE_OPTIONS before the Gradle JVM starts.
bugs.fix: Exported NODE_OPTIONS="--no-deprecation" in gradlew before JVM launch; all node subprocesses (including yarn invoked via ExecOperations.exec) inherit it

bugs.error_message: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: relation "user_sterilization_locations" does not exist
bugs.root_cause: UserSterilizationLocations (and also UserShelters, PasswordResetTokens, EmailChangeTokens) were defined in Models.kt but missing from the listOfTables list in DatabaseFactory.kt, so SchemaUtils.create() never created those tables
bugs.fix: Added UserShelters, UserSterilizationLocations, PasswordResetTokens, EmailChangeTokens to the listOfTables list in DatabaseFactory.kt

bugs.error_message: Uncaught ReferenceError: com is not defined at shelters:49:45
bugs.root_cause: Kotlin/JS IR bundle exports module as globalThis.frontend (UMD), not globalThis.com. Inline script called com.adoptu.frontend.Common.initDropdowns() but the correct path is frontend.com.adoptu.frontend.Common.initDropdowns()
bugs.fix: Changed inline JS calls from com.adoptu.frontend.Common.* to frontend.com.adoptu.frontend.Common.* in both Shared.kt (commonScripts) and EmailVerificationPage.kt

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block | Also: let credential;; try {

bugs.error_message: duplicate key value violates unique constraint user_passwords_pkey (user_id=238913); FK violations on user_active_roles, photographers, temporal_homes, user_shelters, user_sterilization_locations, pets
bugs.root_cause: INSERT INTO users used auto-generated IDs, but all subsequent inserts used hardcoded sequential IDs 1-50. Password INSERT selected ALL users including pre-existing ones.
bugs.fix: Added explicit IDs (1-50) with OVERRIDING SYSTEM VALUE to users INSERT + ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING. Scoped password SELECT to WHERE id BETWEEN 1 AND 50 + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Added ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to roles, photographers, temporal_homes, user_shelters, user_sterilization_locations. Added setval() to advance sequence.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block

bugs.error_message: The operation either timed out or was not allowed. See: https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/#sctn-privacy-considerations-client.
bugs.root_cause: navigator.credentials.get() rejects with NotAllowedError on user cancel; the WebAuthn.kt null-credential check only runs on resolve, never on rejection. LoginPage.kt catch block used error.message directly, which includes the full raw browser string.
bugs.fix: Check error.name == "NotAllowedError" in LoginPage.kt setupPasskeyButton catch block; show "Passkey sign-in was cancelled." instead of the raw message.
